subject: [PATCH v2 061/110] ext4: replace PRIino with %llu/%llx format strings

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of %llu/%llx format strings in ext4 code, specifically that they are not used consistently throughout the codebase. The author agrees to restructure the code in v2 to ensure consistency and fix any issues.
sentiment_signals: acknowledged a need for further work

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara approved the patch and added a Reviewed-by tag, indicating that he has reviewed the code.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch replaces the use of PRIino format macro in ext4 code with concrete format strings %llu and %llx, which are suitable for u64 type inode numbers. This change is necessary because PRIino has been removed from the kernel. The patch updates various files in the ext4 directory to reflect this change.
